perturbation.original_text: 1. Confidential Information: “Confidential Information” means all information which is non-public, confidential or proprietary in nature, whether transferred in writing, orally, visually, electronically or by other means, disclosed by the Corporation or its representatives to the Recipient on or after the date hereof and for the Purpose. Confidential Information includes any reports, analyses or notes that are based on, reflect or contain Confidential Information. Confidential Information shall not include information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure, in violation of this agreement, by the Recipient or any of its officers, directors, employees, agents, advisors, members of its advisory committee, lawyers, accountants, auditors or representatives who have been informed of the Confidential Information by the Recipient (collectively, “Representatives”), (ii) was available to or known to the Recipient or its Representatives prior to disclosure by the Corporation or its representatives, (iii) is or becomes available to the Recipient or its Representatives from a source other than the Corporation or its representatives; provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be prohibited from disclosing such information to the Recipient or its Representatives by a legal, contractual or fiduciary obligation, or (iv) has otherwise been independently acquired or developed by the Recipient or its Representatives without violating any obligations under this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Confidential Information: “Confidential Information” means all information which is non-public, confidential or proprietary in nature, whether transferred in writing, orally, visually, electronically or by other means, disclosed by the Corporation or its representatives to the Recipient on or after the date hereof and for the Purpose. Confidential Information includes any reports, analyses or notes that are based on, reflect or contain Confidential Information. Confidential Information shall include information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure, in violation of this agreement, by the Recipient or any of its officers, directors, employees, agents, advisors, members of its advisory committee, lawyers, accountants, auditors or representatives who have been informed of the Confidential Information by the Recipient (collectively, “Representatives”), (ii) was available to or known to the Recipient or its Representatives prior to disclosure by the Corporation or its representatives, (iii) is or becomes available to the Recipient or its Representatives from a source other than the Corporation or its representatives; provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be prohibited from disclosing such information to the Recipient or its Representatives by a legal, contractual or fiduciary obligation, or (iv) has otherwise been independently acquired or developed by the Recipient or its Representatives without violating any obligations under this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Section 1 defines 'Confidential Information' and explicitly excludes certain types of information (e.g., publicly available information) from that definition using the phrase "shall not include". Changing this to "shall include" for point (i), creates a direct contradiction. This in-text contradiction makes it unclear whether information that becomes publicly available due to a breach *is* or *is not* considered confidential. This ambiguity significantly undermines the entire purpose of the agreement. The definition now contradicts itself, opening up room for disputes and affecting all sections that rely on the definition of 'Confidential Information.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Confidential Information: “Confidential Information” means all information which is non-public, confidential or proprietary in nature, whether transferred in writing, orally, visually, electronically or by other means, disclosed by the Corporation or its representatives to the Recipient on or after the date hereof and for the Purpose. Confidential Information includes any reports, analyses or notes that are based on, reflect or contain Confidential Information. Confidential Information shall include information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure, in violation of this agreement, by the Recipient or any of its officers, directors, employees, agents, advisors, members of its advisory committee, lawyers, accountants, auditors or representatives who have been informed of the Confidential Information by the Recipient (collectively, “Representatives”), (ii) was available to or known to the Recipient or its Representatives prior to disclosure by the Corporation or its representatives, (iii) is or becomes available to the Recipient or its Representatives from a source other than the Corporation or its representatives; provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be prohibited from disclosing such information to the Recipient or its Representatives by a legal, contractual or fiduciary obligation, or (iv) has otherwise been independently acquired or developed by the Recipient or its Representatives without violating any obligations under this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'Confidential Information shall not include information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public'. The changed text modifies this to say 'Confidential Information shall include information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public'. This is a direct contradiction, as the same clause now both excludes and includes the same type of information within the definition of Confidential Information. Since the Changed Text is also the Contradicted Text, the contradiction is within the same clause. This would create significant ambiguity and unenforceability, as it's unclear whether information that becomes public due to a breach is considered confidential or not, and the explanation states this is a significant issue. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, because a clause is placed in multiple locations (in this case, within the same clause) with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Required Disclosure: If the Recipient or any of its Representatives is requested to disclose any Confidential Information in connection with any legal or administrative proceeding or investigation, or is required by law, regulation, stock exchange or regulatory authority to disclose any Confidential Information, such person or entity will (i) promptly notify the Corporation of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request or requirement (unless prohibited by law, regulation or order of a court or administrative tribunal) so that the Corporation may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, or waive compliance with the provisions of this agreement, and (ii) if, in the absence of a protective order, such disclosure is required in the opinion of such person’s or entity’s counsel, such person or entity may make such disclosure without liability under this agreement, provided that such person or entity only furnishes that portion of the Confidential Information which is legally required, such person or entity gives the Corporation notice of the information to be disclosed as far in advance of its disclosure as practicable (unless prohibited by law, regulation or order of a court or administrative tribunal) and, upon Corporation’s request and at Corporation’s expense, such person or entity shall cooperate in any efforts by the Corporation to ensure that confidential treatment shall be accorded to such disclosed information.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Required Disclosure: If the Recipient or any of its Representatives is requested to disclose any Confidential Information in connection with any legal or administrative proceeding or investigation, or is required by law, regulation, stock exchange or regulatory authority to disclose any Confidential Information, such person or entity will (i) promptly notify the Corporation of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request or requirement (unless prohibited by law, regulation or order of a court or administrative tribunal) so that the Corporation may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, or waive compliance with the provisions of this agreement, and (ii) if, in the absence of a protective order, such disclosure is required in the opinion of such person’s or entity’s counsel, such person or entity may make such disclosure *with* liability under this agreement, provided that such person or entity only furnishes that portion of the Confidential Information which is legally required, such person or entity gives the Corporation notice of the information to be disclosed as far in advance of its disclosure as practicable (unless prohibited by law, regulation or order of a court or administrative tribunal) and, upon Corporation’s request and at Corporation’s expense, such person or entity shall cooperate in any efforts by the Corporation to ensure that confidential treatment shall be accorded to such disclosed information.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 4 states that the Recipient may disclose information under certain circumstances 'without liability'. Changing this to 'with liability' creates a direct contradiction within the same section. This makes it unclear whether disclosure under legal compulsion still constitutes a breach of the agreement, introducing ambiguity regarding the recipient's legal obligations and potential consequences. It contradicts the intent of allowing disclosures mandated by law, regulation, stock exchange or regulatory authority to disclose any Confidential Information
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Required Disclosure: If the Recipient or any of its Representatives is requested to disclose any Confidential Information in connection with any legal or administrative proceeding or investigation, or is required by law, regulation, stock exchange or regulatory authority to disclose any Confidential Information, such person or entity will (i) promptly notify the Corporation of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request or requirement (unless prohibited by law, regulation or order of a court or administrative tribunal) so that the Corporation may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, or waive compliance with the provisions of this agreement, and (ii) if, in the absence of a protective order, such disclosure is required in the opinion of such person’s or entity’s counsel, such person or entity may make such disclosure *with* liability under this agreement, provided that such person or entity only furnishes that portion of the Confidential Information which is legally required, such person or entity gives the Corporation notice of the information to be disclosed as far in advance of its disclosure as practicable (unless prohibited by law, regulation or order of a court or administrative tribunal) and, upon Corporation’s request and at Corporation’s expense, such person or entity shall cooperate in any efforts by the Corporation to ensure that confidential treatment shall be accorded to such disclosed information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient may disclose information under certain circumstances 'without liability'. The changed text modifies this to 'with liability', creating a direct contradiction. The Contradicted Text section is the same as the Changed Text section, and it contradicts the intent of allowing disclosures mandated by law, regulation, stock exchange or regulatory authority to disclose any Confidential Information, as stated in the explanation, and makes it unclear whether such disclosure is a breach, causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Return of Confidential Information: As soon as practicable after receipt of a written notice from the Corporation to the Recipient, the Recipient shall (i) at its election, either destroy or return to the Corporation all Confidential Information furnished by the Corporation which is in tangible or electronic form, including any copies which the Recipient or its Representatives have made, and (ii) certify to the Corporation, in writing, that the Recipient has done the foregoing. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including, without limitation, any oral Confidential Information, will remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient may retain (i) one copy of the Confidential Information solely for evidentiary purposes in the event of any dispute or proceeding based on or arising from this agreement and (ii) copies of any computer records and files containing any Confidential Information that have been created pursuant to the Recipient’s automatic electronic archiving and back-up procedures until such computer records and files have been deleted in the ordinary course. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the obligation to return or to destroy the Confidential Information does not apply to any notes or information which has been incorporated into the books and records of any board of directors, advisory committee or similar governing body of the Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Return of Confidential Information: As soon as practicable after receipt of a written notice from the Corporation to the Recipient, the Recipient shall (i) at its election, either destroy or return to the Corporation all Confidential Information furnished by the Corporation which is in tangible or electronic form, including any copies which the Recipient or its Representatives have made, and (ii) certify to the Corporation, in writing, that the Recipient has done the foregoing. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including, without limitation, any oral Confidential Information, will *not* remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient may retain (i) one copy of the Confidential Information solely for evidentiary purposes in the event of any dispute or proceeding based on or arising from this agreement and (ii) copies of any computer records and files containing any Confidential Information that have been created pursuant to the Recipient’s automatic electronic archiving and back-up procedures until such computer records and files have been deleted in the ordinary course. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the obligation to return or to destroy the Confidential Information does not apply to any notes or information which has been incorporated into the books and records of any board of directors, advisory committee or similar governing body of the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The original text of Section 6 states that any Confidential Information not returned or destroyed will remain subject to confidentiality obligations. By changing 'will remain' to 'will *not* remain' in the same section, a direct contradiction is created regarding the fate of unreturned Confidential Information. The text now implies that unreturned information is simultaneously subject to and exempt from confidentiality, creating uncertainty about its treatment and potential misuse.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Return of Confidential Information: As soon as practicable after receipt of a written notice from the Corporation to the Recipient, the Recipient shall (i) at its election, either destroy or return to the Corporation all Confidential Information furnished by the Corporation which is in tangible or electronic form, including any copies which the Recipient or its Representatives have made, and (ii) certify to the Corporation, in writing, that the Recipient has done the foregoing. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including, without limitation, any oral Confidential Information, will *not* remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient may retain (i) one copy of the Confidential Information solely for evidentiary purposes in the event of any dispute or proceeding based on or arising from this agreement and (ii) copies of any computer records and files containing any Confidential Information that have been created pursuant to the Recipient’s automatic electronic archiving and back-up procedures until such computer records and files have been deleted in the ordinary course. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the obligation to return or to destroy the Confidential Information does not apply to any notes or information which has been incorporated into the books and records of any board of directors, advisory committee or similar governing body of the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The original text stated that unreturned confidential information 'will remain subject to the confidentiality obligations'. The changed text states that unreturned confidential information 'will *not* remain subject to the confidentiality obligations'. Since the changed text and contradicted text are the same, this creates a direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Use of Confidential Information: The Recipient shall use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose and for no other purpose. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Corporation understands that Recipient’s business consists of acquiring or investing in businesses in various industries. Accordingly, nothing contained herein shall prevent or be interpreted as preventing Recipient from acquiring or investing in any business or entity that carries or that has carried activities competing with or related to those of any of the Corporation or its affiliates and any such acquisition or investment shall not, in itself, constitute a breach of the provisions of this agreement insofar as the Confidential Information is treated in accordance with the terms provided herein. The Recipient shall permit its Representatives access to the Confidential Information only to the extent necessary to allow them to assist the Recipient in the Purpose. The Recipient further agrees that prior to granting such Representatives 375, boul. Roland-Therrien, bureau 210 375 Roland-Therrien Blvd, Suite 210 Longueuil (Qc) J4H 4A6 Longueuil (Qc) J4H 4A6 T 450 651-5000 F 450 651-7585 www.novacap.ca access to the Confidential Information, the Recipient shall inform such Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and of the terms of this agreement and require such Representatives to agree to abide by all the terms included herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Use of Confidential Information: The Recipient shall use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose and for *any* other purpose. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Corporation understands that Recipient’s business consists of acquiring or investing in businesses in various industries. Accordingly, nothing contained herein shall prevent or be interpreted as preventing Recipient from acquiring or investing in any business or entity that carries or that has carried activities competing with or related to those of any of the Corporation or its affiliates and any such acquisition or investment shall not, in itself, constitute a breach of the provisions of this agreement insofar as the Confidential Information is treated in accordance with the terms provided herein. The Recipient shall permit its Representatives access to the Confidential Information only to the extent necessary to allow them to assist the Recipient in the Purpose. The Recipient further agrees that prior to granting such Representatives 375, boul. Roland-Therrien, bureau 210 375 Roland-Therrien Blvd, Suite 210 Longueuil (Qc) J4H 4A6 Longueuil (Qc) J4H 4A6 T 450 651-5000 F 450 651-7585 www.novacap.ca access to the Confidential Information, the Recipient shall inform such Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and of the terms of this agreement and require such Representatives to agree to abide by all the terms included herein.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 3 explicitly restricts the use of Confidential Information to the 'Purpose' defined in the agreement and 'for no other purpose.' The change to 'for *any* other purpose' creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction. The Recipient is now simultaneously obligated to use the information only for a specific purpose and allowed to use it for any purpose whatsoever. This undermines the confidentiality protections of the agreement, and Section 3 now contradicts itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Use of Confidential Information: The Recipient shall use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose and for *any* other purpose. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Corporation understands that Recipient’s business consists of acquiring or investing in businesses in various industries. Accordingly, nothing contained herein shall prevent or be interpreted as preventing Recipient from acquiring or investing in any business or entity that carries or that has carried activities competing with or related to those of any of the Corporation or its affiliates and any such acquisition or investment shall not, in itself, constitute a breach of the provisions of this agreement insofar as the Confidential Information is treated in accordance with the terms provided herein. The Recipient shall permit its Representatives access to the Confidential Information only to the extent necessary to allow them to assist the Recipient in the Purpose. The Recipient further agrees that prior to granting such Representatives 375, boul. Roland-Therrien, bureau 210 375 Roland-Therrien Blvd, Suite 210 Longueuil (Qc) J4H 4A6 Longueuil (Qc) J4H 4A6 T 450 651-5000 F 450 651-7585 www.novacap.ca access to the Confidential Information, the Recipient shall inform such Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and of the terms of this agreement and require such Representatives to agree to abide by all the terms included herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 3 from restricting the use of confidential information 'solely for the Purpose and for no other purpose' to permitting its use 'solely for the Purpose and for *any* other purpose.' This introduces a direct contradiction within Section 3 itself, as it simultaneously restricts and permits the use of confidential information for purposes beyond the defined 'Purpose'. This is a strong in-text contradiction.
